string 2: That's Junomon's temple! Talk about bright...

So that's where the Digimon who watches over the gate is...
Do you think... Junomon will let us go back to our world...?
Let's go back to our world, all three of us.{next}
Protecting civilians is ADAMAS's mission!{next}
I'll make sure you get back to your dad.{next}
Thank you for saying that, {player}.

We'll be there soon. There should be someone there waiting
for us.
There you are! I'm glad you all made it!

We've been hearing stories about your wins out there!
You sure are something else!
Even Junomon can't wait to meet you!
Especially you!
You guys are getting ahead of yourselves. Reel it in, would you?
Oops... Sorry about that, Minerva.
Yeah, our bad.
Come on. Let's go. Junomon's waiting for us up ahead.

So this is the Great Guardian! Well met, Aegiomon. You have done well
to return to this world.
So, too, has Minervamon informed me of your many feats in battle.

Surely they are but a mere glimpse of the Great Guardian's
true capabilities. A fortuitous day, indeed...
Huh? Wh-What's all this "Guardian" stuff about?!
Well, that egg I was looking for... had already hatched.

And you said you saw the egg when you first opened your eyes...
I mean, I've seen your mind-blowing power, myself.
No doubt about it. You're the Great Guardian, born from the egg.
Here and now, I officially acknowledge you as the Great Guardian.

Now, then, Great Guardian Aegiomon... Allow me to proceed
directly to the main topic at hand.

Listen well, for I shall regale you with the legend of the hero, 
Chronomon.
I exist as custodian of this tale.

All in the hopes of properly guiding the Great Guardian
toward its noble cause upon advent into this world.
Iliad was once safeguarded by two beings of absolute power:

The Wise Warden Homeros, overseer of law and order; and the
Great Guardian Chronomon, protector of Iliad's inhabitants.

The presence of these two beings allowed for an extended
period of peace and prosperity.

Yet one day, the wicked forces of the Titans conspired to
take over Iliad, launching an assault on our domain.

In order to protect our people, Chronomon engaged the
Titans on the field of battle.

After a fierce and terrible conflict, the Titan forces were
routed, driven back into the very depths of the earth.
But Chronomon suffered wounds most fatal.

Surrounded by the grieving populace, Chronomon made
a declaration with his last breath...

...but his will would pass onto another guardian when
the peace of Iliad was once again threatened by evil.
Chronomon then perished, leaving behind a single egg.

That egg would come to be enshrined at the seat of the Wise
Warden Homeros, in a temple symbolizing the peace of Iliad.

Thus would the people of Iliad await the eventual revival
of the Great Guardian...
Our care of the legendary egg was unerring, undeviating.

The Titans have of late begun making incursions into every 
corner of our Digital World, Iliad.

We must protect the world from them, O Great Guardian.
For this purpose, and this purpose alone, were you born.
For you are the successor to Chronomon.

A-All this "Chronomon" and "Great Guardian" stuff...
No matter how you explain it, I just don't get it...
...I see.

In that case... allow me to turn the topic to you humans. 
I am told you wish to return to your original world, yes?
Um, yes, that's right...

I am loath to inform you that opening the gate is not so
simple a task.

If the gate is opened with the Digital World in such a
state, the human world will most certainly be affected.

In the past, the conflict of Digimon has brought harm to
the world of man.

Events of three years past forced more stringent limits 
upon the gate's operation.
Three years past...?

If you wish to open the gate, you must aid in the efforts of
the Great Guardian... your "Aegiomon."

Once peace has been restored to this Digital World and the
people of Iliad, I should be able to open the gate.

I'd be sticking with him no matter what. After all, that's
what we promised back on that starry night in Shinjuku.
Besides... There's something in this world... I need to find out.
Inori...

Now, then, O Great Guardian... I implore you to head for
the Abyss Area at once.

We have confirmed Titan forces have invaded the area. 
I beseech you to return the calm to our seas.

I want you to go and see for yourselves. See just how dire our 
need for the Great Guardian has become.
Would you indulge me thus, Aegiomon?
Y-Yeah, I can... indulge, I think.
My friends can't get back home if I don't, from the sound of it.

I leave it to you, Great Guardian Aegiomon. And you as well,
children of man...

You may ride Locomon to the Abyss Area.
Now, please proceed to the station.
